next_step_text: 50% Probability 4th May- reached out to Zee fr lunch and meeting with Globe about Agentic AI 16th Mar - Sent an intro mail and requesting for a revival meeting. 14th Jan Push Zee now for submission of our proposal in next exco meeting end Jan 30th Oct Zee has advised management (exco) have decided to delay for 1 quarter 27th Oct Revised proposal- to meet Zee week of 12th Nov 14th Oct Zee indicates

competitive_position.summary: Live but frozen two-horse race: Zycus vs SAP Ariba, down-selected 3 Jun 2025. SAP Ariba is the single strongest current vendor threat because it is SAP-native and ACEN's 'significant' SAP secondary-integration cost (Zee, 22 May 2025) hands Ariba a TCO edge on a SAP ERP shop. The larger threat than any vendor is do-nothing / repeated exco deferral. The in-house-built P2P system is the incumbent being displaced. Coupa, GEP Worldwide and iValua sit on the Salesforce field but never recur in calls - faded earlier-round names.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Acen has confirmed that Zycus is shortlisted as top 2. (Other one is SAP-Ariba).
competitors.how_we_win: Neutralize the SAP secondary-integration-cost narrative with a quantified TCO/ROI model and lead with Merlin AI risk-scoring plus non-SAP-locked configuration.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / project deferral
competitors.quote: President Zee has confirmed that due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum.
competitors.how_we_win: Re-establish a compelling event and an exco-funded budget path; the bigger threat than any vendor is repeated deferral.

competitors.name: In-house developed P2P system (incumbent)
competitors.quote: 70% of my spend is non purchase order... there's a manual disconnect.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the ~70% non-PO disconnect and manual supplier onboarding as the cost of standing still.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Not raised in any call; monitor only.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Not raised in any call; monitor only.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Not raised in any call; monitor only.

champion_strength.source: SF Next_Step__c 6 Mar / 27 Feb / 30 Oct 2025; SF activity 24 Mar 2026; Avoma call 13 Aug 2025
champion_strength.summary: Zee Pervez was a strong, active executive champion through Feb-Oct 2025 (insider RFP-template request 6 Mar 2025, 'backing Zycus' 27 Feb 2025) but has been silent ~8 months and unresponsive to 2026 outreach; Jeff De Sagun is an engaged operational driver, not an exec advocate. Champion is at risk and is the deal's root stall.
champion_strength.champion: Zeeshan Pervez (President ACEN Shared Services), with Jeff De Sagun operationally

sources: Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zee names CFO Jonathan Back, 4-person decision group)
SF Next_Step__c 27 Feb 2025 ('CFO Jonathan Back backing Zycus')
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified but never directly engaged on a recorded call. Jonathan Back (CFO and Chief Strategy Officer, SF Executive Sponsor role) holds final budget: on the 28 Feb 2025 call Zee said 'my boss, CFO of the corporation is a Brit, Oxford grad, from JPMorgan' and that 'my CFO is going to be very laser sharp' on the non-PO payment requirement, and SF Next_Step__c 27 Feb 2025 records 'Zee, his boss (CFO Jonathan Back) are backing Zycus.' Zee (President ACEN Shared Services) controls the commercial conversation day to day and told us 'I'm part of that management team... 4 people who are gonna take the decision' (28 Feb 2025). The gap is that Back has never appeared on a call and the budget was explicitly not planned for 2025 (Zee, 28 Feb 2025), so re-confirming his sponsorship and the funded budget path is the open EB action.

recommended_moves: Carl re-engages Zee directly with a dated revival meeting tied to the Agentic-AI and Globe reference hook.
Ask Jeff De Sagun for the current status of the architecture-mapping, reference and legal gates.
Build a quantified TCO/ROI model that answers the SAP secondary-integration cost head-on.
